headline: Medical Officer Contract System Leaves 23,000 Doctors in Limbo
context: Over 23,000 contract medical officers remain without permanent positions or clear career pathways in the public system.

cards.big: 23,000 contract doctors in government service with no guaranteed permanent appointment
cards.sub: Contract system introduced in 2016 now affects majority of junior medical officers

cards.big: Contract doctors earn 20% less than permanent officers with identical workloads
cards.sub: Pay disparity and job insecurity contribute to 15% annual attrition rate

cards.big: Only 3,200 of 23,000 contract doctors were absorbed into permanent positions in 2025
cards.sub: 14% conversion rate means most contract doctors face years of uncertainty

cards.big: Hartal Doktor Kontrak movement in 2021 saw thousands of doctors protest contract terms
cards.sub: Government promised resolution but systemic issue persists 4 years later

cards.big: A healthcare system built on disposable doctors cannot provide stable patient care
Should all medical officers be given permanent positions after completing 2 years of service
